title: Pan-Seared Sirloin Steak with Garlic Pasta
description: A classic combination of tender sirloin steak served with garlic infused pasta. This dish is perfect for a romantic dinner or a special occasion.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
Season the sirloin steak with salt, pepper, and thyme.

Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the sirloin steak for 3-4 minutes per side, or until it reaches your desired level of doneness. Transfer the steak to a plate and let it rest.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until fragrant.
Add the cooked pasta to the skillet with the garlic and toss to combine. If the pasta seems dry, add a bit of the reserved pasta water.
Slice the sirloin steak against the grain and serve with the garlic pasta. Top with grated parmesan cheese if desired.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
